data.title: JPMorgan Knew Actual Number of Customers Before Buying Frank, Defense Lawyer Reportedly Contends at Fraud Trial
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase conducted extensive due diligence into college financial aid startup Frank, and was aware of the actual number of customers before acquiring it in 2021, the lawyer for Charlie Javice, the startup's founder, said in opening arguments at the federal fraud trial of Javice and another former Frank executive, according to multiple news outlets late Thursday.

The US Department of Justice is alleging Javice defrauded JPMorgan into acquiring Frank for $175 million by misrepresenting the platform's customer base, claiming 4.25 million users when it had only about 300,000, Reuters reported, citing prosecutor's opening remarks in Manhattan federal court Thursday. JPMorgan reportedly realized the discrepancy when it tried to contact Frank's customers but received far fewer responses than expected.

Javice's lawyer contends the bank developed "buyer's remorse" after financial aid regulations changed. He accused the bank of using fraud claims against Javice to back out of the deal, Reuters reported.

Javice faces federal criminal charges of securities fraud, wire fraud, bank fraud, and conspiracy, but has pleaded not guilty, according to reports.

JPMorgan did not immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Upbeat on Zurich Insurance Forecasts After FY24 Results Beat
data.description:
Zurich Insurance Group reported "good 2024 figures," with the company delivering a record profit that marginally exceeded consensus expectations, AlphaValue/Baader Europe said Thursday.

As such, the research firm is currently reviewing its forecasts, with an upward revision expected. The group's stock is rated at add, with a target price of 654 francs.

"Net income attributable to shareholders of $5.8bn for 2024 was 3% above the consensus forecast of $5.6bn. BOPAT RoE of 24.6% for 2024 was at a record level and ahead of the target of >20%. Zurich therefore confirmed its new financial targets for 2025-2027, which it announced in November 2024," analysts said.

The insurer's solvency was also robust, with a Swiss Solvency Test ratio estimated at 252%, surpassing its 160% target.

data.title: Oil Prices Ease as Winter Cold Abates and U.S. Inventories Swell
data.description:
Oil prices moved lower early on Friday, dropping on weaker demand and robust supply as bitter winter cold in the northern United States fades and inventories rise.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil for April delivery was last seen down US$0.56 to US$71.92 per barrel, while April Brent crude was down US$0.53 to US$75.95.

Oil traders are adjusting to the start of a bearish outlook for the commodity, as U.S. winter demand eases following the end of a polar vortex that brought bitterly cold weather to northern states, raising demand and cutting supply as infrastructure froze. Demand growth from China, the No.1 importer remains weak and OPEC+ is sticking with a plan to gradually return 2.2-million barrels per day of production cuts to market starting in April, while Western hemisphere supply is growing.

The Energy Information Administration on Thursday reported commercial crude inventories rose by a more than expected 4.6-million barrels last week, though inventories of refined products dropped due to refinery maintenance and winter demand.

"With an elongated winter, a Distillate draw and an increase in product demand the refinery turnaround period is starting to leave a hole in product supply with utilization only running at 85%," PVM Oil Associates noted.

The prospect of easing sanctions on Russian exports as U.S. President Donald Trump warms to Vladimir Putin, his Russian counterpart, while turning against further support for Ukraine is also unsettling markets. Trump is also looking to boost U.S. production, which is already at a record 13.5-million barrels per day, while demand may fall if he follows through on plans to impose widespread tariffs on U.S. imports.

"The market also has to deal with an increasingly erratic message flow from Washington, which on one hand has raised the prospect of increased US production, while on the other causing concerns about the outlook for global growth and demand," Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, noted.

data.title: SECURE Waste Infrastructure's Q4 Earnings Below Analyst Forecasts
data.description:
SECURE Waste Infrastructure reported Friday fourth-quarter 2024 earnings and revenues that came in below analyst expectations, but also said despite "challenges", its business "remains highly resilient".

The company booked a net income of $34 million or $0.14 per share, missing the FactSet analyst consensus forecast of $0.22 and 42% lower than $59 million recorded in the previous year.

Adjusted EBITDA fell to $117 million from $162 million while revenue stood at $332 million, below the FactSet analyst consensus estimate of $357.3 million.

Based on the current economic environment and underlying economic trends, the corporation for its 2025 outlook reiterated its adjusted EBITDA guidance of $510 million-$540 million, with the mid-point reflecting a 10% increase over 2024 pro forma.

The company also expects to maintain a quarterly dividend of $0.10 per share, as it expects the business to remain resilient despite economic uncertainty linked to tariffs.

"Our pipeline of infrastructure projects, combined with our recent metals recycling acquisition, strengthens our network and enhances our ability to transform waste into value for customers and create sustainable returns for shareholders," said Allen Gransch, President and CEO.

data.title: ONESPAWORLD HOLDINGS Ltd SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
OneSpaWorld Holdings Ltd, a leading provider of health, fitness, beauty, and wellness services onboard cruise ships and in destination resorts, has released its annual 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive health and wellness market.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $895.0 million, increased 13% compared to the previous year driven by a 4% increase in Revenue Days, a 4% increase in guest spend, and fleet expansion.

Income from Operations: $78.1 million, representing 8.7% of Total Revenue, an increase from 6.8% in the previous year, reflecting improved operational efficiency.

Net Income: $72.9 million, a significant improvement from a Net Loss of $3.0 million in the previous year, primarily due to a positive change in fair value of warrant liabilities and reduced interest expense.

Net Income Per Diluted Share: $0.69, compared to a Net Loss Per Diluted Share of ($0.03) in the previous year, reflecting the overall improvement in financial performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company earns the majority of its revenue through long-term agreements with cruise line partners, providing health, fitness, beauty, and wellness services onboard ships. These agreements typically last between three to 8.6 years, with an average duration of approximately five years.

Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, the company operated health and wellness centers on 199 ships and in 50 land-based destination resorts. The health and wellness centers served over 26 million guests in 2024.

New Product Launches: The company continues to innovate with new value-added services and products, including medi-spa services such as Dysport, Restylane, CoolSculpting, and Thermage, which are performed by medically licensed professionals.

Geographical Performance: The company operates destination resort health and wellness centers in various countries, including the United States, Maldives, Malaysia, and the Bahamas, among others, with a total of 50 centers as of December 31, 2024.

Operational Expansion: The company has expanded its fleet by adding new ships and renewing agreements with existing cruise line partners, including Azamara, Norwegian Cruise Line, Royal Caribbean Cruises, and Celebrity Cruises.

Future Outlook: The company expects to benefit from the cruise industry's growth, with a consistent pipeline of new ships commissioned by cruise line partners. By the end of 2026, 19 new ships are expected to be introduced by existing partners.

Operational Strategy: The company focuses on enhancing health and wellness center productivity through initiatives such as increasing pre-booking and pre-payment capture rates, expanding targeted marketing, and utilizing technology for dynamic pricing.

Competitive Advantage: The company maintains a significant competitive advantage through its unmatched global infrastructure, extensive staff recruiting and training platform, and long-standing relationships with cruise line and destination resort partners.

Strategic Initiatives

OneSpaWorld Holdings Limited has focused on expanding its operations by increasing the number of health and wellness centers onboard cruise ships and in destination resorts. The company has also invested in innovative service offerings and proprietary technologies to enhance guest experiences and increase revenue. Additionally, OneSpaWorld has entered into a new credit agreement to refinance existing debt, providing more favorable terms and enhancing financial flexibility.

Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital structure by repurchasing common shares and paying dividends. In 2024, OneSpaWorld repurchased 1,395,432 common shares from Steiner Leisure Limited and 745,302 shares under a Share Repurchase Program. The company declared quarterly dividends of $0.04 per common share in 2024. Furthermore, OneSpaWorld refinanced its debt by entering into a new $100 million Term Loan Facility and a $50 million Revolving Facility, which replaced the previous First Lien Term Loan Facility.

Future Outlook: OneSpaWorld plans to continue leveraging its strategic partnerships with cruise lines and destination resorts to drive growth. The company aims to enhance its service offerings and expand its market presence. With the new credit facilities in place, OneSpaWorld is well-positioned to pursue further expansion opportunities and maintain financial stability. The company also intends to continue its share repurchase program and dividend payments, subject to board approval and financial performance.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces significant risks from actual or threatened epidemics, pandemics, and outbreaks of illnesses, which can adversely impact business operations, financial condition, and results of operations. These health crises can lead to government-imposed travel restrictions, reduced consumer confidence, and disruptions in global credit and financial markets, affecting the company's ability to borrow and increasing counterparty credit risks. Additionally, the company is dependent on agreements with cruise lines and destination resort health and wellness centers, which can be terminated with limited notice, posing a risk to revenue streams.

The cruise industry, a major revenue source, is vulnerable to economic changes, consumer confidence fluctuations, and operational disruptions such as accidents, environmental violations, and severe weather conditions. The company is also exposed to risks from increased costs, including fuel and commodity prices, which can impact financial results. Furthermore, the company relies on key officers and qualified employees, and any loss of these personnel could materially affect business operations.

Regulatory risks include potential adverse changes in U.S. or foreign tax laws, which could increase tax liabilities. The company is also subject to environmental, health, safety, security, data privacy, and anti-corruption laws, with non-compliance potentially leading to litigation, fines, or penalties. The increasing scrutiny and changing expectations regarding ESG policies may impose additional costs or expose the company to risks.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the evolving demands for health and wellness services and the need to innovate continuously. The company aims to enhance collaboration with partners, expand pre-marketing platforms, and employ data-driven strategies to optimize revenue. However, the success of these strategies depends on external factors such as consumer trends and economic conditions.

The company is exposed to market risks, including currency fluctuations, which can impact financial results due to payments made in foreign currencies. Additionally, the company faces risks from potential cyberattacks and data breaches, which could disrupt operations and damage reputation. The use of artificial intelligence presents challenges related to security and regulatory compliance, with potential impacts on business operations.

SEC Filing: ONESPAWORLD HOLDINGS Ltd [ OSW ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: IDEXX LABORATORIES INC /DE SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
IDEXX Laboratories Inc., a leading provider of diagnostic and information management-based products and services for the veterinary, water testing, and livestock industries, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive market environment.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $3,897.5 million, reflecting a 6.5% increase driven by growth in CAG Diagnostics recurring revenue and higher volumes in the Water business.

Gross Profit: $2,378.9 million, representing a 61.0% margin, up from 59.8% in the prior year, due to favorable business mix and price realization offsetting inflationary costs.

Income from Operations: $1,128.3 million, with a margin of 29.0%, slightly down from 30.0% in the previous year, impacted by increased operating expenses.

Net Income: $887.9 million, benefiting from increased revenues and operational efficiencies.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through three primary business segments: Companion Animal Group (CAG), Water, and Livestock, Poultry, and Dairy (LPD). CAG includes diagnostic and information management-based products and services for the companion animal veterinary industry. Water focuses on testing solutions for microbiological parameters in water. LPD provides diagnostic tests and services for livestock and poultry health and dairy quality.

Geographical Performance: Approximately 22% of the company's consolidated revenue for 2024 was derived from products manufactured or sourced in U.S. dollars and sold internationally in local currencies. The company maintains a sales presence in major regions worldwide, including Africa, Asia Pacific, Canada, Europe, and Latin America.

Sales Units: The installed base of premium diagnostic instruments as of December 31, 2024, includes 74.1 thousand Catalyst units, 51.8 thousand Premium Hematology units, and 21.3 thousand SediVue units.

New Product Launches: In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company launched the IDEXX inVue Dx Cellular Analyzer in North America, which uses advanced optics and AI technology in a slide-free, load-and-go platform.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates a decline in U.S. same-store clinical growth levels in 2025 due to near-term sector and macroeconomic dynamics. The company is focused on enhancing veterinary practice efficiency and expanding its diagnostic offerings to support growth.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on expanding its Companion Animal Group (CAG) segment by increasing the installed base of diagnostic instruments and enhancing its software and services offerings. This includes the launch of new products like the IDEXX inVue Dx and upgrades to existing systems to improve veterinary practice efficiency. Additionally, the company is pursuing growth in its Water segment through international sales expansion and regulatory approvals, and in the Livestock, Poultry, and Dairy (LPD) segment by offering differentiated tests for disease management.

Capital Management: The company repurchased approximately 1.74 million shares of its common stock in 2024, with plans to repurchase approximately $1.5 billion worth of shares in 2025, subject to market conditions. It maintains a $1.25 billion Credit Facility with $998.1 million available for borrowing, and has $250 million outstanding on a Term Loan. The company also plans to pay off its 2025 Series C and B Notes with available cash or new financing. No dividends were declared or paid, and the company has no current plans to initiate dividend payments.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued investment in its strategic initiatives, including a $160 million capital expenditure plan for 2025 focused on manufacturing and software development. It plans to manage its debt levels while maintaining flexibility for future growth opportunities. The company expects to manage its capital structure to support share repurchases and potential new financing to fund strategic objectives.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Risks: The companion animal healthcare industry is highly competitive, with increasing competition from existing and new entrants. The company's ability to maintain growth and profitability depends on successfully executing strategies such as developing innovative products, maintaining premium pricing, and expanding sales and marketing activities.

Supply Chain Risks: Dependence on third-party suppliers for components and raw materials poses a risk. Disruptions in the supply chain could negatively impact the company's ability to supply products, leading to decreased sales and higher costs.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to various U.S. and foreign regulations that could limit or delay product marketing and sales. Compliance with evolving regulations, especially those related to AI and environmental standards, could increase operational costs.

Technological Risks: The integration of AI into products presents risks, including potential flaws in AI algorithms and datasets, as well as regulatory challenges. Failure to address these issues could harm the company's reputation and slow AI adoption.

Operational Risks: The company relies on the continuous operation and security of its IT systems. Cybersecurity breaches or IT disruptions could adversely affect business operations and reputation.

Market Risks: Economic weakness or perceived economic weakness in key geographies could reduce demand for products and services, impacting sales and profitability.

Environmental Risks: Climate change and related regulations could disrupt operations and supply chains, increasing costs and affecting revenues.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by increased competition and regulatory changes. Strategies to address these include investing in R&D for innovative products, enhancing supply chain resilience, and expanding international operations to mitigate market risks.

Market Risk Mitigation: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect financial results. Hedging activities are in place to mitigate these risks, but they may not fully offset the impact of unfavorable currency movements.

SEC Filing: IDEXX LABORATORIES INC /DE [ IDXX ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

Onex Swings to Q4 Loss of US$2 Million
data.description:
Onex , an investment management firm, swung to a fourth-quarter net loss of US$2 million, or US$0.02 per share, compared to year-ago net earnings of US$373 million, or US$4.81 per share.

Private equity investments had net gains of US$11 million, down from US$250 million, the company said on Friday. Investments in credit strategies generated net gains of US$16 million, dropping from US$66 million.

Onex said it had about US$8.3 billion of investing capital, or US$113.70 per diluted share, as of Dec. 31, 2024. Its investing capital per fully diluted share returned 6% for 2024 or 15% in Canadian dollars.

Over the last five years, investing capital per fully diluted share has had a compound annual return of 13%, the company said.

The board declared a first-quarter dividend of US$0.10 per subordinate voting share, payable April 30 to shareholders of record on April 10.

"Shareholders continue to benefit from our strong balance sheet and liquidity position, and most recently through our substantial issuer bid," President and CEO Bobby Le Blanc said.

data.title: Tiziana Life Sciences Teams Up With Renaissance Lakewood on Nasal Drug Delivery
data.description:
Tiziana Life Sciences said Friday it has signed an agreement with contract development and manufacturing firm Renaissance Lakewood to produce foralumab in a nasal device.

Tiziana said it is evaluating intranasal foralumab in a phase 2a study to potentially treat neurodegenerative and inflammatory conditions.

Financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

data.title: Coinbase Stock Rises. The SEC Is Giving Up Its Case Against the Crypto Exchange. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Coinbase shares were rising early Friday after the cryptocurrency exchange said the Securities and Exchange Commission was ready to drop its lawsuit against the company.

Coinbase said Friday that enforcers at the SEC will recommend dismissal of a two-year-old lawsuit which sought to regulate the company as a stock exchange.

"SEC staff have agreed to dismiss their case against us (pending Commission approval)," the company said in a post on social-media site X.

Coinbase shares were rising 4.3% in premarket trading.

Dismissing the Coinbase lawsuit still requires a commission vote. The company expects that to happen next week, according to Coinbase's chief legal officer Paul Grewal, The Wall Street Journal reported.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Camping World Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.125 a Share, Payable March 27 to Holders of Record as of March 14
TomaGold Provides 2023 Drilling Program Results on Obalski Property
data.description:
TomaGold on Friday said latest results from its 2023 drilling program on the Obalski property, located 2km south of Chibougamau, Quebec, revealed a new north-south trending polymetallic zone.

The company said the program consisted of six drill holes of 1,539.9 meters, with an objective to demonstrate the presence of a north-south trending polymetallic mineralized system initially identified by two holes drilled in 2017.

The results suggested the presence of a new north-south trending zone (020 degrees) that dips to the east between -50 degrees and -60 degrees, which will require further work to better define its potential.

"According to the technical committee's recent review of the Obalski project, there are possibly several of these north-south structures. The main recommendation is to carry out targeted geophysical surveys along its 020-degree structures, followed by additional drilling," said TomaGold VP Exploration of Jean Lafleur.
